name: presidents-of-war
description: Michael Beschloss's Presidents of War — presidential history toolkit examining how American commanders-in-chief wielded war powers from 1807 to modern times and the evolution of the imperial presidency
useCase: Understand the evolution of presidential war powers, learn from key wartime presidencies, analyze crisis decision-making, and recognize patterns of executive war-making
knownRisks: The book is focused on the American presidency — its analysis may not apply to other political systems
Beschloss writes from a constitutional perspective that may not suit readers with different political philosophies
The book was published in 2018 — it does not cover the most recent presidential actions
